授業目的: This course emphasizes that accounting students, having been exposed to the methodology and practice of accounting, need to examine the broader implications of financial accounting theories for the fair and efficient treatment of accounting items and their reporting. The course aims to give the students a critical awareness of the current financial accounting and reporting environment, taking into account the diverse interests of both internal and external users of financial information.
到達目標: Upon successful completion of the course, students will be able to acquire a deeper understanding of major accounting theories, including information asymmetry, stakeholder, legitimate, and efficient contracts, understand accounting under ideal conditions, understand the efficient securities market, gain knowledge on the value relevance of accounting information, enhance knowledge of measurement and an efficient contracting approach to decision usefulness, and determine executive compensation and detect earnings management.
特記事項: 【合併】Accounting Theory and Practice in North America（国際学研究科前期）/Accounting Theory and Practice　（経営戦略研究科）
関連科目: Basic Accounting, Financial Reporting and Analysis, Advanced Topics in Accounting, International Accounting
授業方法: This is a face-to-face format class. The course combines lectures, assignments, quizzes, and a final report to ensure assurance of learning.

第1回: Face-to-face class: Introduction and overview of the course: Review of major accounting theories.
